document_description: Academic paper reporting water quality measurements for river, drain, wastewater, or related aquatic samples.
extraction_scope: Extract the main quantitative water quality measurements from the paper's most relevant tables or figures and normalize them to one measurement per record.
record_label: one measured water quality parameter value from a relevant table in the paper
records_description: Normalized water quality measurements extracted from relevant tables in the paper, with one record per parameter value.
extraction_instructions: Prefer tables over running text whenever the same values appear in both places.
Extract one record per measured parameter value, not one wide row containing many pollutants.
Preserve the location or station label exactly as shown in the table.
Preserve the sampling period exactly as shown in the paper, whether it is a month, season, year, campaign, stretch, or another grouping.
Use parameter_name for the pollutant or water quality indicator exactly as shown or in a clean standardized form close to the paper wording.
Put the numeric result in value and the reported measurement unit in unit.
If the paper reports ranges, mean plus minus SD, min-max, or similar summary statistics, keep the main numeric value in value and explain the summary format in notes.
If a table contains several pollutants for the same location and period, create separate records for each pollutant.
preferred_table_signals: water quality or physicochemical parameter table
headers containing pollutant names, parameter names, indicators, or units
tables with locations, stations, sites, reaches, drains, or sampling points
captions or context about river water quality, sewage, pollution monitoring, or seasonal/monthly observations
fallback_sources: figures or graphs that clearly label location, time grouping, parameter, and numeric axis values
supplementary tables or appendix tables referenced as part of the same study dataset
narrative text only when it directly reports the same measurement values and no clearer table is available

fields.label: Location
fields.description: Sampling location, station, site, drain, reach, or monitoring point name.
fields.normalization: Preserve the site label as written in the paper unless a trivial case normalization is needed.

fields.label: Sampling period
fields.description: Time or grouping label associated with the measurement, such as month, season, year, campaign, pre-monsoon, post-monsoon, or another reported grouping.
fields.normalization: Keep the grouping label exactly as reported instead of converting months, seasons, and years into one format.

fields.label: Parameter
fields.description: Name of the water quality parameter, pollutant, microbial indicator, nutrient, or physicochemical variable.
fields.normalization: Use a clean parameter name close to the paper wording, but avoid expanding abbreviations unless the paper clearly defines them.

fields.label: Value
fields.description: Main numeric value reported for the parameter.

fields.label: Unit
fields.description: Measurement unit exactly as reported in the paper, such as mg/L, ug/L, MPN/100 mL, CFU/100 mL, or pH units.
fields.normalization: Copy the unit exactly as reported when possible.

fields.label: Statistic
fields.description: Statistic or value type if specified, such as mean, median, range, min, max, SD, or average.

fields.label: Sample matrix
fields.description: Sample type if explicitly reported, such as river water, wastewater, effluent, surface water, or groundwater.

fields.label: Source table
fields.description: Table number, caption, or other short identifier of the source table if available.

fields.label: Notes
fields.description: Short note about ambiguity, merged cells, transformed values, or other extraction details when needed.
